frontmatter.title: Silent Meeting
frontmatter.subtitle: Everyone writes simultaneously in a shared doc — then we read and discuss.

mdx_content:
1
What did you learn in the last 2 days?
2
What surprised you?
3
What confused you?

Information ExtractionWithout a central BI tool, executives can drop raw spreadsheets into an AI and instantly query data without waiting for analytics teams. Inventory & LogisticsQuickly parse sales vs. stock data to identify slow-moving inventory before it becomes a liability.
The Operational MultiplierEquip your team to do the same workflows in half the time—from summarizing vendor negotiations to reconciling mismatching invoices.

Conversational CommerceSearch intent matching: "I need an outfit for a summer wedding in Dhaka" instead of rigid keyword filters. Automated Catalog TaggingAI looks at product photos and automatically generates metadata (color, fabric, occasion), saving hours of manual entry.

Generative Display AdsGenerate 100 variants of ad creative for different demographics instantly. Customer Service TriageAutomate the first 80% of support tickets ("Where is my order?") by connecting to the order database.

These models don't just read text. They see images, read PDFs, understand code, and process audio.
Images Paste a photo of a whiteboard and ask it to extract the action items. Documents Upload a 50-page PDF and ask for a summary, or find a specific clause. Code Paste an error message and it explains what went wrong and how to fix it. Spreadsheets Upload an Excel file and ask it to analyze trends or write formulas.

"Fluent in over 6 million forms of communication."
Think of the LLM as C-3PO.
He reads the documentation for every system, then speaks computer on your behalf.
You say"Schedule a meeting with Asan for Tuesday at 2pm" Claude readsGoogle Calendar API docs, parameters, auth, time zones Claude doesCalls the API, creates the event, confirms it is done.
Browse the web. Read files. Run code. Send emails.
Any tool with an API, Claude can use.

An LLM is not a search engine. Its knowledge is frozen. So how does it learn new things?
The Problem
Hallucinations It might make things up confidently. You won't know unless you check.
The Fix
Live Browsing Claude searches the web and reads actual sources in real-time.
Without browsing
"PriyoShop's revenue grew 40% last quarter."
✗ Might be completely made up.
With browsing
Claude searches the web, reads news sources, and gives you a real answer.
✓ Verified data with citations.

Every provider has small, medium, and large models. We use the frontier tier — the most capable — because quality matters for business decisions.
Claude By Anthropic. Strong at writing, analysis, and following complex instructions. ChatGPT By OpenAI. The most well-known all-rounder with a large tool ecosystem. Gemini By Google. Deeply integrated with Google Workspace. Strong at data and search.

Today's expensive model becomes next year's budget model.
Think smartphones — today's budget phone is better than last year's flagship. AI is on the same trajectory, but moving faster.
2023 GPT-4 costs $30/million tokens. State of the art. 2025 Models 10x cheaper at equal quality. Flash/Haiku tiers. 2026+ What costs $100/month today will cost $10. Then $1.
Product IntegrationInference costs fraction of a cent. We can generate **display ad creative** at scale and generate product description variants for A/B testing instantly.

Protecting the Bank & Keys Engineers must not paste database credentials, API keys, or raw profit margins into free consumer tools (like free ChatGPT), which use data for training.
Enterprise Isolation Enterprise tools and API accounts guarantee 0-day retention and ring-fence proprietary business logic.

Rule #1Never paste actual customer PII (Personally Identifiable Information) into any public LLM. Rule #2Confidential P&L, inventory margins, and vendor negotiations stay exclusively in approved Enterprise/Team tiers.

🎙️
Whisper to your phone
Voice input is 3—5x faster than typing. Talk to Claude like a colleague.
🔄
Think out loud
Ramble, correct yourself, add context. Claude handles messy input perfectly.
🌍
Any language works
Speak in Bangla, English, or mix. Claude understands both fluently.
⚡
Voice → text → edit
Dictate a rough draft, then ask Claude to clean it up and format it.

Take a photo of something on your desk — a document, a receipt, or your business card — and paste it into Claude.
Try these prompts:
→ "What does this say?"
→ "Summarize this document"
→ "Extract all the numbers from this image into a table"
→ "Translate this to English"

CSV / TSVSales data, ERP exports, transaction logs ExcelUpload .xlsx directly — formulas and sheets PDFContracts, invoices, and complex reports Plain TextEmails, meeting notes, copy-paste snippets
📄
Drag & drop
or paste with ⌘V / Ctrl+V
CSV • XLSX • PDF • TXT

→

Pro tip: Tell Claude the format — "Give me bullet points" or "Write it as an internal memo."

01
Start anywhere
Use ChatGPT, Gemini, or Claude to brainstorm an idea or draft a prompt.
02
Copy the output
Take the AI's response — even if it's rough — and paste it into a different AI.
03
Ask AI to improve it
"Make this prompt better" or "Write a more detailed version of this instruction."
04
Cross-pollinate
Different AIs have different strengths — use one to check or enhance another's work.

You say:
"Write me a prompt that will help me analyze my weekly sales data."
Claude generates:
"You are a sales analyst for PriyoShop. Compare this week's CSV to the previous week. Flag any region that dropped more than 10%..."
→ Consistent, high-quality output every time.
Meta-prompting"Write me a prompt for..." is the most underused AI technique. Save & reuseOnce it works, save it. Build a personal AI toolkit. IterateRefine until perfect: "That's good, but also add a section for..."

You say
"Interview me about my department's biggest bottleneck."
→
What happens
AI asks targeted questions → you discover root causes you hadn't articulated.
You say
"Ask me questions to write my performance review."
→
What happens
AI pulls out accomplishments and metrics through guided Q&A.
You say
"Stress-test my board presentation assumptions."
→
What happens
AI stress-tests your logic and helps you rehearse tough questions.
Key phrase: "Don't write it for me — ask me questions first."

AI says
"I can't browse"
Reality
It can still analyze any data you paste. Most tasks don't need live web access.
You respond
"Analyze this data I'm pasting manually."
AI says
"I can't do that"
Reality
It's being cautious. Rephrase, or break the task into smaller steps.
You respond
"Let's start with just the first step."
AI says
"Not enough info"
Reality
Give it permission to make assumptions, then correct what's wrong.
You respond
"Make your best guess. I'll correct it."

The context window is everything the AI can see at once — your message, history, and any files you uploaded.
Think: whiteboard Everything on it is visible. When it fills up, old content gets erased or ignored. Size matters Claude has a 200K token window — roughly 150,000 words. A massive amount of space.

Context windows are getting bigger every year. Soon you will be able to paste entire codebases or full novel-length histories.
2023 ~8K tokens (GPT-3.5). A few pages of text. 2024 ~200K tokens (Claude 3). A full novel. 2025+ 1M+ tokens. Entire project histories.
The "Full Bucket"As you chat back and forth, the window fills up. Eventually, the AI forgets the initial instructions provided at the start.

Research shows models pay the most attention to the beginning and end of the context. The middle gets less attention.
Start Middle End (Recent)
This is why important instructions work best at the beginning or end of your message.

In practice, context limits are manageable. Just follow a few simple rules:
One task per chat Don't mix marketing with finance. Start a new chat for each distinct task. Good hygiene If a conversation gets messy, start fresh. Copy key context into a new chat. Growing fast Windows are doubling every few months. Soon this won't be a constraint. Claude tells you You'll see a warning when you're running low. That's your cue to refresh.

frontmatter.title: Quiz: When to Start a New Chat?

mdx_content:
Scenario 1
You finished a marketing draft. Now you want to analyze sales.
→ New chat. Different task, different data.
Scenario 2
Claude is forgetting earlier instructions in a long thread.
→ New chat. Copy key context and start fresh.
Scenario 3
You're refining a report and iterating on specific wording.
→ Keep going. Same task, iterating is fine.
Scenario 4
You need an offer letter, then a JD, then interview questions.
→ Keep going. Related tasks, shared context helps.

Update Your Mental Model ContinuouslyAI models improve rapidly. Don't let old assumptions limit you. If a task failed months ago, try again with the latest model. Keep a log of struggles to re-test after updates. Avoid Frontloading FilesDon't manually add many files you *think* are relevant. This pollutes the context window. Instead, let the model search the codebase to find the most relevant files itself. Beware of Code BiasClaude Code can get stuck in existing (and potentially bad) coding patterns. To get a fresh perspective, have it implement a new feature in a clean, empty folder first, then integrate the new code into your main project.

Thank You.

Let's build something extraordinary together.
Alex Miller — fotoflo@gmail.com calendly.com/fotoflo/30min

frontmatter.title: Context is King: Optimize for Peak Performance

mdx_content: Modularize Your CodebaseBreak down large files into smaller, focused modules. This reduces the amount of code the AI needs to read for each edit, keeping the context window lean. Regularly ask the AI to identify and refactor files over a certain line count. Offload Tasks to SubagentsAggressively delegate tasks like online research, codebase exploration, and log analysis to subagents. This keeps the main context clean and focused, allowing you to stay within the optimal 0-50% context window range for longer. Use Hierarchical CLAUDE.md FilesStructure instructions in nested `CLAUDE.md` files within different project subfolders. The AI only loads the most relevant files for a task, ensuring instructions are progressively disclosed and the context remains sharp.

frontmatter.title: Claude Code Power User: 60+ Tips for Mastery

mdx_content: The Only Limitation is Your ImaginationClaude Code is a versatile tool that can be applied to nearly any problem, from video editing and fixing minor life annoyances (like auto-reconnecting to Wi-Fi) to complex tasks like data visualization, automating communication, and even helping you find a girlfriend by reverse-engineering APIs. The Core Value of AIAI's power lies in its ability to sift through massive amounts of data quickly and align its actions to your specific goals, unlike traditional software algorithms that may work against you. Embrace the 'Creative Mode' MindsetUsing Claude Code feels like playing life on 'Minecraft creative mode.' Adopt an experimental and creative approach to problem-solving to unlock its full potential.

Run Parallel Sessions Like a GameTreat your work like the game 'Factorio.' Run 3-5+ Claude Code instances per project in parallel. This ensures there's always an agent needing attention, turning your focus from single-task speed to pipeline design and bottleneck identification. Integrate with ObsidianUse Obsidian for notes, as Claude Code excels at navigating local Markdown files. It can create backlinks, generate graph views, and more. Leverage the Obsidian CLI via a skill for even greater power and context savings. Dictate Prompts, Don't TypeUse an accurate voice-to-text tool like HyperWhisper to dictate prompts. This is significantly faster than typing, especially when managing multiple parallel sessions.

Use a Hierarchical StructureLayer your configurations: use a root user-level file for global rules, project-level files for project specifics, and `local.md` files (gitignored) for machine-specific settings (e.g., 'You are running in WSL'). Practice Progressive DisclosureInstead of one giant `CLAUDE.md`, create smaller, focused files in subfolders (e.g., `/backend`, `/macos`). This ensures only the most relevant instructions are loaded, keeping the context sharp. Reference External FilesKeep your `CLAUDE.md` files concise by referencing longer instructions, like custom vocabularies or library quirks, in separate files. This is great for complex packages the model struggles with. Keep it LeanWhen the model makes a mistake, update the most relevant `CLAUDE.md`. Every time a new model is released, audit your files and remove instructions that are no longer necessary. Avoid adding bloated prompts from social media.

{/* Header Section */}
Operation Manual // v2.0.4
Control Protocols
{/* Grid Layout */}
{/* Verbose Mode */}
/VERBOSE
Enable verbose true to expose the model's inner monologue. Observe the logic chain to identify prompt optimizations.
{/* Lexicon / Language */}
/LEXICON
Adopt the agent's internal terminology—like 'CDN download URL'—to minimize translation overhead and increase hit rate.
{/* Clarifying Questions */}
/PLAN
Force the model to generate 20 clarifying questions before any code is written. Flush out edge cases in the discovery phase.
{/* Rewind Tool */}
/REWIND
Do not argue with hallucinations. Use /rewind to prune the context tree and pivot from a clean state.
{/* Footer Detail */}
System-Level Access Required
